personalInfo.name: Manish Mohan Srivastava
personalInfo.title: Operations Head — Program & Operations Leadership
personalInfo.summary: Seasoned operations leader with over 20 years of experience driving operational efficiency, program & revenue management for pharma and healthcare tech clients. Expertise in program management, Lean/Six Sigma-driven process optimization, and leading large distributed teams to deliver measurable revenue & margin improvements. Proven track record in scaling complex operations and managing multi-million dollar revenue portfolios.

experience.title: Operations Head / Sr Manager Client Services / Associate Director
bullets: Directed and oversaw a large-scale operational team of **300+ resources**, including Team Leads and Project Managers across India.
Supporting a network of over **50,000 patients**, doctors, and nurses.
Managed a substantial revenue portfolio of **USD 5 million** while driving impressive **gross margins of over 30%**.
Spearheaded the implementation and execution of **Patient Support Programs** for major pharmaceutical clients.
Developed and executed strategic initiatives to optimize resource allocation and drive revenue growth.
Responsible for identifying and mitigating potential risks and resolving escalated issues.

experience.title: Account / Program Manager
bullets: Managed a diverse portfolio of projects spanning **seven countries** across the Asia-Pacific region (Australia, NZ, Japan, China, Taiwan, Macau, India).
Led cross-functional teams throughout the entire project lifecycle, ensuring projects aligned with client objectives.
Ensured consistent achievement of client **Service Level Agreements (SLAs)** through proactive monitoring.
Analyzed business requirements documents (BRDs) and reviewed design specifications.

experience.title: Operations & Business Development Support
bullets: Supported organizational objectives by actively participating in a family-owned network marketing business.
Contributed to team building and business development initiatives.
Aided in establishing teams and business operations.

experience.title: Quality Program Manager (IT)
bullets: Managed end-to-end **Revenue Cycle and SLA Management** for a team of **1400-1500 FTEs** across multiple locations in India (2012-2014).
Directly supervised a team of **140 FTEs** in Provider Network Operations as Deputy Manager (2010-2012).
Enhanced efficiencies across teams through **Lean and Six Sigma projects**, resulting in annual cost savings.
Championed a **Green Belt project** focused on enhancing form-level accuracy.
Led a Lean project targeting **latency reduction** in internal systems.

experience.title: Team Lead
bullets: Directed a team of **20 telecallers** in a high-volume call center supporting the Capital One Credit Cards program.
Monitored and coached team performance to optimize service delivery and customer engagement.
Focused on achieving key **Service Level Agreements (SLAs)** related to customer satisfaction and AHT.

experience.title: Business Development Manager
bullets: Drove business growth for a facilities management company, securing new accounts and generating substantial revenue.
Successfully achieved the turnover goal within the first year.
Targeted potential clients and strengthened client relationships.

experience.title: Head Quality Control Department
bullets: Led the Quality Control Department, ensuring production aligned with specifications and quality benchmarks.
Collaborated with production teams to implement quality control processes and inspections.
Minimized defects and improved product consistency.

experience.title: Sr. Executive P&A Department
bullets: Managed payroll and administrative functions, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards.
Overseeing employee compensation processes, including salary disbursement, ESI, PF, and bonuses.
Contributed to streamlined HR operations and enhanced employee satisfaction.

caseStudies.title: Patient Support Program Implementation
caseStudies.problem: Scaling operational support for major pharma clients across a distributed healthcare network.
actions: Optimized resource allocation using strategic initiatives.
Ensured adherence to strict quality standards and budget parameters.
caseStudies.outcomes: Supported 50,000+ patients/doctors with USD 5M revenue and 30%+ gross margins.

caseStudies.title: Revenue Cycle & SLA Revamp
caseStudies.problem: Managing large-scale revenue cycle operations across multiple locations with stringent SLAs.
actions: Implemented Lean/Six Sigma driven process optimizations.
Established clear communication channels and stakeholder management.
Automated reporting for real-time SLA tracking.
caseStudies.outcomes: Stabilized delivery with a [ADD EXACT %/VALUE] increase in efficiency and achieved 100% SLA compliance.
